host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a 64 MiB image passed through
change: Added commitInPlaceDirectoryCreate: the full-tree COW analogue of the legacy rewrite-based createImageOnlyRootDirectory. The new directory takes the volume's next object id and is appended to the preserved full tree; the rebuilt fs-tree gains the directory inode + its dirent, root's valence grows by one, and the new volume directory count (0xC0) + next object id advance (no data, no extent-ref change). Threaded a directoryCountDelta through the COW chain and finalizeFsCommit. Extracted recoverPreservedFiles (shared by buildChainedFileList and the directory create). Exposed as commitImageOnlyDirectoryCreate + the CLI command commit-image-directory-create.

linux_apfsck.with_content: apfsck -cw clean on create-directory into a container that already holds a directory and child (only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' note)
linux_apfsck.empty_container_divergence: create-directory on a freshly-formatted (genesis) empty container draws an apfsck 'Chunk-info block: xid is too recent.' error, but the live spaceman, the chunk-info block, and the nx_superblock are byte-identical to the kernel-accepted file-insert case (which apfsck passes). This is a new apfsck-vs-Apple divergence (apfsck false-positive); the macOS kernel/fsck_apfs accept the container - see below.

apple_fsck_apfs.container: 64 MiB image, volume CDTEST: a single empty directory 'solo' created on a freshly formatted container (the apfsck-erroring case)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel mounted CDTEST read-write; ls -la /Volumes/CDTEST showed the created directory 'solo' (drwxr-xr-x), and listing /Volumes/CDTEST/solo was empty
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 3; the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 5
apple_fsck_apfs.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk1 (unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: apfs-cow-dircreate-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict), apfs-cow-dircreate-kernel-read.png (kernel ls showing the created directory)

note: Remaining A2-3.2: the other three directory mutations on the COW engine (delete-directory, directory-child write, directory-child delete), the raw entry points, and wiring the File Management bridge's directory routes off the legacy raw writers.
